instructions.instruction: NEVER use `locator.click()` to click elements inside the CloudWatch iframe. ALWAYS use `page.mouse.click(x, y)` with page-level coordinates.
instructions.relevant_when: Interacting with any element inside the CloudWatch microConsole iframe via Playwright.
instructions.why_given: locator.click() times out after 5s on cross-origin iframe elements. page.mouse.click() with absolute coordinates works reliably.

instructions.instruction: ALWAYS use `frame.evaluate()` to query or manipulate CloudWatch DOM content. NEVER use `page.evaluate()` for elements inside the iframe.
instructions.relevant_when: Reading or modifying any DOM element inside the CloudWatch iframe.
instructions.why_given: page.evaluate() runs in the outer shell document where CloudWatch selectors return null.

instructions.instruction: To scroll the CloudWatch results table, ALWAYS use `frame.evaluate(() => document.querySelector('.logs-table__wrapper').scrollTop = N)`. NEVER use `window.scrollBy()`.
instructions.relevant_when: Scrolling through log results in the CloudWatch Logs Insights results table.
instructions.why_given: window.scrollBy() scrolls the outer shell page, not the results table which has its own overflow container.

instructions.instruction: ALWAYS screenshot the CloudWatch alarm detail page when verifying an alarm. NEVER use the raw AWS/Lambda Errors metric graph as proof.
instructions.relevant_when: Capturing screenshots to verify or document a CloudWatch alarm.
instructions.why_given: AWS/Lambda Errors = 0 does not mean no alarm triggered — the alarm may be driven by a Logs metric filter, not the native metric.

instructions.instruction: Dismiss cookie banners and new-experience banners before navigating in a fresh AWS Console session.
instructions.relevant_when: Opening a new AWS Console tab via SSO or after cookie consent is required.
instructions.why_given: Undismissed overlays block navigation and cause screenshot captures to include irrelevant UI elements.
